basics.name: Qian Taowen
basics.summary: BSc student in Mathematics and Artificial Intelligence at the University of Toronto. Research interests span computer vision, applied mathematics, and AI-driven content creation.

location.address: Heneng Fengdanlishe Residence, Baiye Road, Guitang Subdistrict

work.summary: Summer internship covering infrastructure, AI-driven logistics, conversational AI, and Unity prototyping.
highlights: Deployed and maintained a NAS (Network-Attached Storage) system on company VMs, configured user permissions, and implemented short-term backup/snapshot solutions.
Optimized supply-demand logistics with AI-driven tools, reducing operational costs in transportation and labor through dynamic resource allocation.
Built a QQ chatbot integrated with the GPT API, adding contextual memory and multimedia support (stickers, images) to improve user engagement.
Prototyped small-scale games in Unity, gaining hands-on experience with C# scripting and 3D environment design.

courses: CSC413 — Neural Networks and Deep Learning
CSC404 — Introduction to Video Game Design

publications.name: Towards Efficient LLMs: Analyzing Computational Bottlenecks and Optimization Strategies
publications.publisher: 2025 3rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Database and Machine Learning (AIDML 2025)
publications.summary: Undergraduate thesis surveying the principal computational bottlenecks in large language models and evaluating modern optimization strategies such as quantization, pruning, attention acceleration, and mixture-of-experts routing. Produced during the USC Viterbi online research project advised by Prof. Saty (DreamWorks Animation).

awards.title: 2nd Place, Best Overall Game
awards.summary: Awarded for Salt, Grooves, and Garlic (Salty Studios, U of T & Centennial College). The Best Overall Game category rewards mechanics, level design, interface, controls, user experience, and emotional impact.

portfolio.name: Salt, Grooves, and Garlic
portfolio.description: Unity action-rhythm game built by a 10+ person team (Salty Studios, U of T & Centennial College). Awarded 2nd place — Best Overall Game at Level Up Showcase 2026 (Presented by Zynga). Programmed the rhythm input system, boss attack patterns, and the dynamic Groove Meter mechanic.

portfolio.name: USC Computer Graphics Research Project
portfolio.description: Online research project at USC Viterbi (advised by Prof. Saty, DreamWorks Animation) on 3D modeling, rendering, and AI-driven content creation using Blender, Maya, RenderMan, and THREE.js. Culminated in the AIDML 2025 paper.

portfolio.name: CSC320 Visual Computing — Project Series
portfolio.description: Four hands-on projects at the University of Toronto: a homography-based document scanner, Beier-Neely image morphing (75% runtime improvement via NumPy vectorization), Criminisi-style exemplar-based inpainting, and a randomized PatchMatch implementation.
